data.title: Electra upsizes at-the-market offering
data.description:
• Electra Battery Materials Corporation (ELBM:CA) said on Friday it has increased the size of its at-the-market equity program to allow for sales of up to $25 million of common shares.
  • The shares may be sold from time to time through H.C. Wainwright at prevailing market prices on the Nasdaq Capital Market.
  • The $25 million aggregate amount includes up to $5.5 million previously covered under a December 2025 prospectus supplement.
  • Electra said it intends to use net proceeds for working capital and general corporate purposes, including expenditures related to commissioning its Ontario sulfate refinery.
  • ELBM -0.0% after hours to $0.838.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: NaaS Technology receives Nasdaq notification
data.description:
• NaaS Technology (NASDAQ:NAAS) announced on Friday it received a notice from Nasdaq on Feb. 17 stating it is not in compliance with the $35M minimum market value requirement for continued listing on the Nasdaq Capital Market, as set forth in Nasdaq Listing Rule 5550(b)(2).
  • Nasdaq calculates MVLS based on total shares outstanding multiplied by the closing bid price and said NaaS has 180 days, until Aug. 17, 2026, to regain compliance.
  • The notice also said that the company fails to meet other Nasdaq standards, including minimum $2.5 million stockholders’ equity and $500,000 net income requirements.
  • The company said it aims to regain compliance by Aug. 17, 2026, and expects its shares to continue trading on Nasdaq during the period.

data.title: Foresight to change ADS ratio to 1-for-3 effective February 26
data.description:
• Foresight Autonomous Holdings (FRSX) said on Friday it will change the ratio of its American Depositary Shares to ordinary shares from 1 ADS representing 30 ordinary shares to 1 ADS representing 90 ordinary shares, effective February 26, 2026.
  • As part of the change, holders will receive one new ADS for every three existing ADSs held. The company’s ordinary shares will not be affected.
  • Foresight said its ADSs will continue trading on Nasdaq under the symbol “FRSX” with a new CUSIP number.
  • The company said it expects the change to increase the ADS trading price proportionally and help maintain compliance with Nasdaq listing requirements.
  • FRSX -4.08% after hours to $0.8057.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: SA Analyst says choppy trading masks a building of strength beneath the surface
data.description:
U.S. equities are poised to climb further into the second quarter, according to Seeking Alpha analyst Alex King of Cestrian Capital Research, who argues that underlying sector rotation is masking building upside potential.

“All the U.S. equity indices closed green on the week,” King noted, emphasizing that recent gains come despite months of sideways, choppy trading in the S&P 500 and Nasdaq Composite. He attributed the consolidation to weakness in software stocks being offset by strength in other areas of the market. In his view, the software group is now under accumulation and could soon provide renewed leadership. 

King added that unless semiconductor shares experience a sharp downturn, broader indices are positioned to advance alongside a recovery in software. “We believe the market will be significantly higher by end Q2,” he said. 

On Friday, Wall Street extended its gains after investors digested a hotter-than-expected Personal Consumption Expenditures inflation report. The Nasdaq Composite rose 0.9%, while the S&P 500 gained 0.6% and the Dow Jones Industrial Average added 0.4%, closing out a broadly positive week for major benchmarks.

Market Tracking ETFs: (DIA), (DDM), (DOG), (DXD), (SDOW), (SPY), (VOO), (IVV), (RSP), (SSO), (UPRO), (SH), (SDS), (SPXU), (QQQ), (QQQM), (TQQQ), (QID), and (SQQQ).

data.title: Amicus Therapeutics Non-GAAP EPS of $0.31, revenue of $634M
data.description:
• Amicus Therapeutics press release (FOLD): FY Non-GAAP EPS of $0.31.
  • Revenue of $634M.
  • Cash, cash equivalents, and marketable securities increased to $293.5 million at December 31, 2025, as compared to $249.9 million at December 31, 2024.

data.title: Capital Clean Energy Carriers prices 250 mln euro unsecured bond offering
data.description:
• Capital Clean Energy Carriers (CCEC) said on Friday it priced 250 million euros of unsecured bonds to investors in Greece.
  • The bonds will mature in 2033 and carry a 3.75% coupon, payable semi-annually.
  • Settlement is expected on February 25, 2026, with trading on the Athens Exchange set to begin on February 26.

  • The company said proceeds will be used to repay debt, fund part of its capital expenditure and for working capital needs. 

  • Estimated offering expenses are about 7.5 million euros.
  • CCEC +0.34% after hours to $23.08.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: IEEPA tariff ruling creates margin tailwind for consumer names
data.description:
U.S. companies that were vulnerable to American trade policy breathed a collective sigh of relief Friday morning when the Supreme Court dealt a setback to President Trump’s trade agenda by invalidating tariffs imposed under the International Emergency Economic Powers Act (IEEPA).

The knee-jerk reaction in the wake of the announcement launched shares of tariff-sensitive sectors higher, before the gains evaporated amid speculation that the White House will find new, and possibly more creative ways to address trade imbalances.

“To be clear, [today’s ruling] specifically impacts the reciprocal IEEPA tariffs, which are just one layer of the overall tariff picture,” RBC Capital’s Steven Shemesh weighed in, noting that the retreat from the post-announcement highs reflects a decision that was largely priced in.

The market saw shares of Nike (NKE), Capri Holdings (CPRI), Wayfair (W), V.F. Corp (VFC), Urban Outfitters (URBN), and Five Below (FIVE) all react accordingly but then end the day off their best levels as SCOTUS’s decision still leaves certain tariffs intact. These include national security tariffs on steel and aluminum, tariffs on China, and any other tariffs enacted under Section 232 of the Trade Expansion Act of 1962.

The ruling also leaves in doubt the nearly $200B already collected, a decision that could take months, along with the fate of recently negotiated trade deals.

Until the White House finds a workaround to getting Congressional approval, the removal of IEEPA tariffs “introduces a meaningful margin tailwind across many consumer discretionary names,” Jefferies analyst Randal Konik wrote in his note to clients.

By employing a weighted average tariff ((WAT)) barometer, Konik has identified certain companies most leveraged to the removal of tariffs.

Sourcing about half of its merchandise from India positions Signet Jewelers (SIG) as the “primary beneficiary,” with its WAT exposure dropping from 15.1% to 0% and earnings expected to reflect the change.

With a 145% total tariff on products made in China and a 10% reciprocal tariff on other countries, Yeti (YETI) had a 14.7% WAT exposure, translating into a $0.35 EPS headwind from tariffs. Through supply chain diversification (which preceded tariffs), Yeti (YETI) set an ambitious goal to move 90% of its drinkware manufacturing outside of China. With the remaining tariff pressure expected to dissipate, Konik sees earnings recovery of as much as $0.70.

By moving roughly 90% of all manufactured goods imported into the U.S. out of China and into several Southeast Asian nations, SharkNinja (SN) mitigated the worst of the trade tariffs but still faced increased costs of as much as 20%, resulting in a 50 basis point headwind to its margins. Konik expects this will now reverse to a 50 basis point benefit.

With more than half its shoes produced in the Southeast Asian nation, the July trade deal between the U.S. and Vietnam cut the company’s trade exposure by more than half and launched Nike (NKE) shares higher. However, the company’s remaining $1.5B cost headwind represented ~320 basis points of gross margin pressure. Konik estimates that the elimination of IEEPA tariff “flips this into a margin tailwind, supporting incremental gross margin expansion through FY26.”

data.title: Vicor outlines plans for record product revenue run rate approaching $800M in 2026 amid strong demand and capacity expansion
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Vicor Corporation (VICR) Q4 2025

Management View

  • CEO Patrizio Vinciarelli described 2026 as “a year of great opportunity for Vicor,” highlighting expectations for record bookings, revenues, and profitability, and a significant increase in utilization of the company’s first chip fab.
  • Philip Davies, Corporate VP of Global Sales & Marketing, stated, “As we exited 2025, book-to-bill ratio increased to over 1.2 in Q4 and has continued to increase in Q1. At the start of 2026, we can say that this will be a year of different challenges and greater opportunities. This should result in record bookings, revenues and profitability and significantly higher utilization of our first chip fab.”
  • CFO James Schmidt reported, “Vicor reported product revenue for the fourth quarter of $92.7 million, up 4.5% from the third quarter total of $88.7 million and up 15.3% from the fourth quarter 2024 total of $80.4 million. Royalty revenue for the fourth quarter totaled $14.5 million, a 33.1% sequential decrease from $21.7 million in the third quarter and a 7.8% decrease from $15.8 million in the fourth quarter of 2024.”
  • Vinciarelli emphasized ongoing IP enforcement, referencing a second investigation by the United States International Trade Commission into “illegal importation of power modules and computing systems, infringing Vicor's IP to nonisolated bus converters.”
  • Davies noted, “Our lead customer for VPD solutions is ramping a Gen 4 factorized power system before transitioning to a Gen 5-based solution with higher current density and performance,” with the Gen 5 transition expected to begin in the second half of 2026.

Outlook

  • Management refrained from providing specific quarterly guidance, citing unpredictability in timing and amounts related to licensing. Schmidt stated, “given that we cannot predict with certainty the timing or amounts of outcomes relating to our licensing practice, we will not provide quarterly guidance.”
  • Davies said, “As we approach high utilization of our first chip fab, we are beginning to engage customers in capacity reservation agreements to secure their supply needs.”
  • Vinciarelli described 2026 as a year of “major increase in product revenue, well above the rate of last year and at a level that we haven't enjoyed for quite some time,” and confirmed the company is targeting a run rate that could utilize 80% of fab capacity, implying product revenue could approach an $800 million run rate.

Financial Results

  • CFO Schmidt reported, “For Q4, we recorded operating income of $15.7 million, representing an operating margin of 14.6%. For the full year 2025, operating income totaled $81.8 million or 18.1% of revenue in patent litigation settlement compared to operating loss of $1.3 million or minus 0.4% of revenue in the prior year.”
  • “Net income for Q3 totaled $46.5 million GAAP diluted earnings per share was $1.01 based on a fully diluted share count of 46,297,000. For the full year 2025, net income increased to $118.6 million from $6.1 million in the prior year. In 2025, fully diluted earnings per share increased to $2.61 from $0.14 in the prior year.”
  • Cash and cash equivalents at quarter-end were $402.8 million, with Q4 operating cash flow of $15.7 million and capital expenditures of $5.5 million.
  • Q4 book-to-bill improved sequentially, coming in well above 1, and 1-year backlog increased 15.8% from the prior quarter, closing at $176.9 million.

Q&A

  • Quinn Bolton, Needham & Company: Asked about fab utilization. Vinciarelli responded, “It's a combination of demand, increasing demand on a number of fronts, not just hand computing... but also in test equipment... and other end markets.”
  • Bolton, Needham & Company: Asked for clarification on IP licensing revenue base. Schmidt confirmed, “the royalty revenue I quoted in my prepared remarks of $57.4 million in 2025 does not include patent litigation settlement.”
  • Jonathan Tanwanteng, CJS Securities: Inquired about VPD customer transitions and fab expansion. Vinciarelli explained, “the Gen 4 system is mature... the next-generation system will provide an additional layer of use of capacity as we get into next year.” He also noted, “we've made 2 offers on area where we could build a facility... No deal done yet, but I would expect that we're likely to do something on this soon.”
  • Richard Shannon, Craig-Hallum Capital Group: Queried on licensing growth and content per XPU. Vinciarelli stated, “we have 2 major licensees. We expect to have a lot more and future contribution from those 2 should become quite a bit larger.” Davies added, “the opportunity for us will be somewhere between $200 to $400 per XPU. But very much depends on... the current that XPU, the number of rails, that type of thing.”
  • Justin Clare, ROTH Capital Partners: Asked about capacity utilization and CapEx for a new fab. Vinciarelli said, “existing fab being well utilized within a year,” and estimated new facility investment at “$250 million, $300 million.”
  • Multiple analysts raised questions about capacity reservations, licensing expansion, and competitive landscape. Management indicated selective engagement and ongoing negotiations for capacity and partnerships.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts’ tone was positive, focusing on growth, capacity, and licensing expansion, with congratulatory remarks on financial performance and strategic progress.
  • Management maintained a confident tone in both prepared remarks and Q&A, repeatedly using phrases such as “we are very confident,” “this should result in record bookings, revenues and profitability,” and “we see a major opportunity for us to grow our licensing business considerably.”
  • Compared to the previous quarter, both management and analysts displayed increased optimism, with attention shifting from uncertainty to executing on escalating demand and scale.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Management’s language evolved from cautious optimism in Q3 to marked confidence in Q4, driven by a significant backlog increase and higher book-to-bill.
  • Guidance remained non-specific, but projections for fab utilization and product revenue run rate became more explicit, with management confirming an $800 million product revenue run rate target for 2026.
  • Analysts in both quarters focused on licensing, fab utilization, and competitive differentiation, but Q4 included more questions about capacity reservation, fab expansion, and customer engagement.
  • Key metrics such as product revenue, gross margin, operating income, and backlog all improved sequentially or year-over-year, while strategic priorities shifted toward scaling capacity and enforcing IP.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited unpredictability in licensing outcome timing as a reason for withholding guidance.
  • Capacity constraints at the first fab and the timing for bringing additional capacity online were discussed as challenges.
  • The competitive landscape for VPD solutions and the need for alternative sources or partners was referenced, with Vinciarelli noting, “we need to, in effect, look at making the most out of the opportunity as opposed to limiting the scope of the opportunity by wanting to do it.”
  • Analysts pressed on customer engagement, capacity reservations, and the potential for competitive alternatives in the market.

Final Takeaway

Management underscored a transformational year ahead, with record bookings, revenues, and profitability anticipated as fab utilization ramps and licensing opportunities expand. The company is targeting a run rate that could bring product revenue close to $800 million, supported by strong backlog, capacity reservations, and ongoing strategic enforcement of intellectual property. With new capacity investments on the horizon and sustained demand across high-growth markets, Vicor’s leadership expressed confidence in delivering substantial growth while navigating evolving market and operational challenges.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: PPL outlines $23B capital investment plan and extends 6%–8% EPS growth target through 2029 amid data center demand surge
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: PPL Corporation (PPL) Q4 2025

Management View

  • Vincent Sorgi, President, CEO & Director, highlighted that “we finished the year exactly where we said we would, delivering safe and reliable electricity and natural gas service to more than 3.5 million customers and achieving our stated financial targets for investors.” He reported ongoing earnings of $1.81 per share, 7.1% growth from the prior year.
  • Sorgi announced an updated business plan: “For 2026, we're issuing ongoing earnings guidance of $1.90 to $1.98 per share, with a midpoint of $1.94 per share, representing 7.2% growth from 2025. We're extending our 6% to 8% annual EPS growth target through at least 2029, expecting the EPS CAGR through 2029 to be near the top end of that range based off of our 2025 ongoing earnings.”
  • The company plans $23 billion in capital investments from 2026 through 2029, an increase from the previous $20 billion plan, targeting grid hardening, modernization, and new generation in Kentucky. “Our updated plan includes the critical investments that strengthen our networks against those more frequent and severe storms and other extreme weather impacts,” Sorgi stated.
  • Sorgi shared regulatory updates, including a Kentucky rate case approval resulting in an aggregate increase of approximately $233 million in annual electric and gas revenues and approved ROEs of 9.775% for both utilities. He also noted progress on Pennsylvania and Rhode Island rate cases, with decisions expected in mid-2026.
  • The Pennsylvania data center pipeline was described as “projects in advanced stages… now total approximately 25.2 gigawatts, up another 23% since our last quarterly update.”
  • On cost efficiency, Sorgi said, “we outperformed our O&M savings target by about $20 million, achieving approximately $170 million in run rate savings from our 2021 baseline, about a year ahead of our $175 million target for the end of 2026.”
  • Sorgi highlighted a joint venture with Blackstone to build generation for data centers: “We have not embedded any earnings contributions or CapEx from the JV in our updated business plan. However, depending on the timing... we could see earnings contributions as early as the back end of our updated planning horizon.”
  • Joe Bergstein, Executive VP & CFO, reported: “On a full year basis, our 2025 GAAP earnings were $1.59 per share compared to $1.20 per share in 2024. Excluding special items, our 2025 ongoing earnings were $1.81 per share, an improvement of $0.12 and in line with expectations.”

Outlook

  • Sorgi stated, “For 2026, we're issuing ongoing earnings guidance of $1.90 to $1.98 per share, with a midpoint of $1.94 per share, representing 7.2% growth from 2025.”
  • PPL is extending its 6% to 8% annual EPS growth target through at least 2029 and expects the EPS CAGR through 2029 to be near the top end of that range.
  • The capital investment plan projects $23 billion from 2026 through 2029.
  • Sorgi noted, “Importantly, beyond this strong base plan, we see several identifiable upside opportunities to further enhance or extend our earnings growth over time.”
  • The company modified its annual dividend growth rate target to 4% to 6% while issuing equity to fund the capital plan.

Financial Results

  • For 2025, PPL achieved ongoing earnings of $1.81 per share and GAAP earnings of $1.59 per share.
  • Bergstein detailed that Kentucky results increased by $0.09 per share, Pennsylvania by $0.04 per share, and Rhode Island decreased by $0.02 per share compared to 2024. Corporate and Other was $0.01 better than last year.
  • For the fourth quarter, Kentucky increased by $0.02 per share, Pennsylvania by $0.01, Rhode Island by $0.01, and Corporate and Other by $0.03 per share versus the same period a year ago.
  • The Board declared a quarterly cash dividend of $0.285 per share, a nearly 5% increase, resulting in an annualized dividend of $1.14 per share.

Q&A

  • Shahriar Pourreza, Wells Fargo, asked for more color on the Pennsylvania rate case process. Sorgi responded, “A lot of discussion around the impact of data centers on customer affordability... the main focus of the hearing. So yes, I would say that was positive.” He added, “From an overall process standpoint, I think the case continues to advance. It's going as we would expect.”
  • Jeremy Tonet, JPMorgan, inquired about Genco contracts and JV auction participation. Sorgi replied, “I don't think we would necessarily need to wait for an earnings call now. I think that would be a significant event that we would do that off cycle for sure.” Regarding auction participation: “We're still evaluating... The special auction that PJM might be holding... perhaps, but we really need to see the final structure.”
  • Steven Fleishman, Wolfe Research, asked about technology types in alternative generation. Sorgi declined specifics but said, “these are technologies that we could get online more in kind of the '28, '29 time frame.”
  • Michael Lonegan, Barclays, questioned the size of upside investment opportunities. Bergstein said, “I'm not going to quantify that today from either an EPS or a capital perspective... ultimately, the size and timing... will depend on a number of factors.”
  • Julien Dumoulin-Smith, Jefferies, asked about JV materiality and load forecasts. Sorgi noted, “I think you'll continue -- you'll see from us ultimately some earnings contribution in the back end of this decade, more meaningful into next.”

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analyst sentiment was neutral to slightly positive, with a strong focus on regulatory clarity, data center pipeline growth, the Blackstone JV, and rate case outcomes. Most questions were probing for further details or timing on strategic initiatives.
  • Management maintained a confident and disciplined tone, emphasizing execution, growth visibility, and risk management. Sorgi repeatedly affirmed, “we feel really good about the underlying strength of our filing and the investments that are in there.”
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management’s confidence was more pronounced, especially in discussing growth opportunities and regulatory progress, while analysts’ tone remained constructive but pressed for more specificity on upside catalysts.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • The updated plan now extends the 6% to 8% EPS growth target through 2029, while the prior quarter only specified through 2028.
  • Capital investment guidance increased from $20 billion over the previous period to $23 billion for 2026–2029.
  • Dividend growth target was adjusted downward to 4% to 6% as the company plans further equity issuance.
  • A new business plan highlighted more robust upside opportunities, especially related to data center growth and the Blackstone JV, which was discussed in more detail than the previous quarter.
  • The tone from management has shifted to greater confidence, supported by regulatory outcomes and visibility into demand growth, while analysts continue to seek details on execution and timing of large initiatives.

Risks and Concerns

  • Sorgi identified the industry-wide challenge: “first quartile T&D performance is trending worse overall for the industry as a result of more frequent and severe storms as well as more extreme weather events.”
  • The company faces potential risks related to the timing and magnitude of data center-driven load growth and the need for corresponding generation investment.
  • Sorgi acknowledged disappointment that the Kentucky commission did not approve the earnings sharing mechanism, leading to reassessment of future rate case timing.
  • Management is monitoring regulatory developments in Pennsylvania and Rhode Island, with outcomes pending in both jurisdictions that could impact financial results.
  • There is ongoing risk around the timing of contracts for the Blackstone JV and securing turbine supply for new generation.

Final Takeaway

PPL Corporation’s management emphasized that 2025 marked a year of delivery, with operational, financial, and strategic targets met and a new business plan extending strong growth guidance through 2029. The company highlighted robust momentum in data center-driven demand, a significant capital investment increase, and disciplined cost management, all while maintaining a focus on customer affordability and credit quality. Management underscored its confidence in the execution and long-term outlook, positioning PPL as a leader in balancing growth opportunities and risk in the evolving energy landscape.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: First Citizens BancShares said to be looking for potential transactions
data.description:
First Citizens BancShares (FCNCA) (FCNCB) is said to be seeking deals, having considered pursuing a deal for KeyCorp (KEY).

The Raleigh-based bank holding company has asked advisers for potential transactions that First Citizens can pursue, people familiar with the discussions told Bloomberg News.

The move comes as First Citizens seeks to vault over a key $250B asset threshold, which comes with increased compliance costs and stricter capital and liquidity requirements, according to the Friday report by Bloomberg.

Generally, lenders look to navigate the new rules with additional profitability and revenue that come with greater scale, the report noted.

First Citizens has reportedly initiated discussions with regulators about the possibility of a major acquisition.
